info.name: Tourism Platform API
info.description: Manual test collection for @tourism/api — built to test FROM ZERO (empty DB). Run top-to-bottom: _SETUP (users) → ADMIN (creates the catalog) → CUSTOMER (browses/books what admin made). No seed data assumed. Each 'create' captures ids/slugs into variables so the next requests just work. See apps/api/postman/README.md.

item.name: _SETUP (Admin API · run once)
item.description:
One-time user setup with the Supabase **service_role** key (`{{supabaseServiceRoleKey}}` — fill from `apps/api/.env` → `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`). ⚠️ This key bypasses RLS — never commit or share it. Pushes the passwords in your env onto the Supabase users so Login works.

• FORGOT password on EXISTING users → run **1**, then **3** + **5**.
• FIRST TIME (no users yet) → run **2** + **4** (password is set on create).

request.description: Lists Supabase Auth users; captures {{customerUserId}} / {{adminUserId}} by matching email. Run first (check the console for the ids).

exec: console.log('customer:', c ? c.id : 'NOT FOUND → run step 2', '| admin:', a ? a.id : 'NOT FOUND → run step 4');

request.description: Creates the customer ALREADY confirmed (no email step). 422 = already exists → use step 3 instead. Captures {{customerUserId}}.

exec: if (pm.response.code < 300) { const u = pm.response.json(); if (u.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('customerUserId', u.id); }

request.description: Resets the EXISTING customer's password to {{customerPassword}} + confirms email. Needs {{customerUserId}} from step 1.
Creates the admin already confirmed. The email must match the API's ADMIN_EMAILS. 422 = exists → use step 5. Captures {{adminUserId}}.

exec: if (pm.response.code < 300) { const u = pm.response.json(); if (u.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('adminUserId', u.id); }

request.description: Resets the EXISTING admin's password to {{adminPassword}} + confirms email. Needs {{adminUserId}} from step 1.

item.name: ADMIN — build the catalog first
item.description: Run this BEFORE the CUSTOMER flow on an empty DB. Auth: Bearer {{adminToken}} (inherited). Order matters: category → destination → tour → departure (each create captures the slug/id the next step needs). Admin email must be in the API's ADMIN_EMAILS.

request.description: Mirror + promote to ADMIN (creates the local users row). The email must be in ADMIN_EMAILS, else 403 NOT_ADMIN.
On an empty DB this returns zeros — that's correct; it verifies admin access works.
A customer hitting an admin route must be 403. (Run after the customer Login.)
Captures {{categorySlug}} (the tour create in 03 needs it).

item.name: DELETE /admin/tour-categories/:slug (409 once a tour uses it)

request.description: Demonstrates the FK guard: once a tour references this category, delete is 409 CATEGORY_HAS_TOURS. Only deletes when unused.
Captures {{destinationSlug}} (the tour create in 03 needs it).
Replace-all. publicId must be a real Cloudinary id (mint via 05 · Media Uploads).
409 DESTINATION_IS_ACTIVE (deactivate first) then 409 DESTINATION_HAS_TOURS (FK). Demonstrates the 3-tier delete policy.

item.name: POST → tourSlug + tourId  ⭐ create after cat+dest

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Hoi An Lantern Walk",
  "summary": "An evening walking tour through the old town.",
  "categorySlug": "{{categorySlug}}",
  "destinationSlugs": ["{{destinationSlug}}"],
  "primaryDestinationSlug": "{{destinationSlug}}",
  "durationDays": 1,
  "maxGroupSize": 16,
  "basePrice": 49,
  "currency": "USD",
  "isPublished": true,
  "suitableFor": ["FAMILY", "COUPLE"],
  "badges": ["BEST_VALUE"],
  "included": ["Guide", "Bottled water"],
  "highlights": ["Old town by night"]
}

request.description: isPublished:true so the CUSTOMER catalog sees it. Captures {{tourSlug}} + {{tourId}}.

exec: if (t) { if (t.slug) pm.collectionVariables.set('tourSlug', t.slug); if (t.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('tourId', t.id); }

request.description: Replace-all media set. publicIds must be real Cloudinary ids (use 05 · Media Uploads first).
409 TOUR_IS_PUBLISHED (unpublish first) then 409 TOUR_HAS_BOOKINGS (FK). Don't run mid-flow — it removes the tour the customer flow uses.
Captures {{departureId}} (the customer booking needs it). startDate must be today-or-future.
409 DEPARTURE_HAS_BOOKINGS once it has a booking. Don't run before the customer booking flow.

purpose ∈ TOUR_HERO | TOUR_GALLERY | TOUR_VIDEO | DESTINATION_HERO | DESTINATION_VIDEO | USER_AVATAR. Returns a signed Cloudinary upload payload; upload the file to Cloudinary, then save its publicId via a media PUT.

item.name: 06 · Reviews Moderation
item.description: Run after the CUSTOMER writes a review.

request.description: Approving (false→true) enqueues a 'review approved' email via the outbox.

item.name: 07 · Enquiries (CRM)
item.description: Run after the CUSTOMER submits an enquiry.

request.description: status ∈ NEW | CONTACTED | QUOTED | WON | LOST.

item.name: 08 · Bookings
item.description: Run after the CUSTOMER has a PAID booking.

request.description: Refunds the customer's booking ({{bookingCode}}). Needs it to be PAID (complete a real checkout first), else 400 BOOKING_NOT_REFUNDABLE.

item.name: CUSTOMER — browse & book what admin made
item.description: Run AFTER the ADMIN flow. Auth: Bearer {{customerToken}} (inherited); public sub-folders override to No Auth.

request.description: Captures access_token → {{customerToken}}.
Creates the local users row from the logged-in identity. REQUIRED before other endpoints can resolve you.
publicId must be a real Cloudinary id (mint via ADMIN → 05 · Media Uploads with purpose USER_AVATAR).

item.name: 01 · Catalog (Public)
item.description: No Auth. Shows the tour ADMIN created. Re-captures {{tourSlug}}/{{tourId}}/{{departureId}} from the public side.

request.description: Books the admin-created tour + departure. Creates a PENDING booking. paymentProvider: STRIPE | PAYPAL. Captures {{bookingCode}}.
Returns { checkoutUrl }. Open it in a browser to pay; the webhook then flips the booking to PAID (needed for review + refund).
PayPal only — after the buyer approves on PayPal's return. STRIPE bookings 400.
Cancels a PENDING booking you own (only run if you don't intend to pay for it).

item.name: POST /reviews (needs a PAID booking you own)

request.description: Only a PAID booking owned by you can be reviewed (else 400/403). Complete checkout (02) + payment first. Captures {{reviewId}}. Duplicate → 409.
tourId is the UUID of the admin-created tour.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Jane Traveller",
  "email": "jane.lead@example.com",
  "phone": "+44 7700 900123",
  "message": "Interested in a family trip to Hoi An in August.",
  "tourId": "{{tourId}}",
  "nationality": "United Kingdom",
  "travelDate": "2026-08-01",
  "groupSize": 4,
  "budgetTier": "$1000-$2000",
  "interests": ["culture", "food"]
}

request.description: Public lead capture (rate-limited 5/min). Returns { received: true }. Then check ADMIN → 07 · Enquiries.
`website` is the honeypot. Still returns 201 (so bots can't tell) but the row is NOT written — verify it never appears in ADMIN → 07.

item.name: _WEBHOOKS (reference)
item.description: Server-to-server payment webhooks. NOT callable by hand — they verify a provider signature over the raw body. To exercise for real: Stripe CLI (`stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/stripe/webhook` + `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed`) or the PayPal sandbox simulator. Documented here for reference only.

body.raw: { "note": "Use Stripe CLI to deliver a signed event; a hand-written body is rejected (400)." }
{ "note": "Use the PayPal sandbox webhook simulator; signature verified against PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID." }
